seed_instruction: Write a email to your professor to ask for an extention on a project due in two days.
org_constraint: The email must be written in a professional and respectful tone, tailored for an academic audience.
new_constraint: Write the email in an informal tone, tailored for a casual audience.
instruction: Write a email to your professor to ask for an extention on a project due in two days. The email must be written in a professional and respectful tone, tailored for an academic audience. Write the email in an informal tone, tailored for a casual audience.
response:
Here are two versions of the email: one professional for an academic setting, and one informal for a casual audience.

### Option 1: Professional & Respectful (Academic Audience)

**Subject:** Extension Request: [Project Name] – [Your Full Name] – [Course Name/Number]

Dear Professor [Last Name],

I hope this email finds you well.

I am writing to respectfully request a brief extension on the [Project Name], which is currently due on [Due Date]. Due to [briefly state reason, e.g., an unexpected personal emergency / an unforeseen conflict with another major assignment / a family matter], I am unable to finalize the work to the standard I aim to deliver by the original deadline.

I am committed to completing this project to the best of my ability. Would it be possible to submit the final draft by [Proposed New Date/Time]? I understand that extensions are granted based on specific circumstances, and I appreciate your time and consideration regarding this request.

Thank you for your understanding.

Sincerely,

[Your Full Name]
[Student ID Number]

***

### Option 2: Casual & Informal (Casual Audience)

**Subject:** Quick question about the [Project Name] deadline

Hi [Professor's First Name],

Hope you're having a good week!

I'm reaching out to see if there's any chance we could push back the deadline for the [Project Name] by a day or two. Something unexpected came up on my end, and I want to make sure I give the project the attention it deserves rather than rushing it in at the last minute.

Would it be okay if I handed it in by [Proposed New Date/Time]? I know it's only two days away, but I'd really appreciate the extra time to get it right.

Thanks so much for understanding!

Best,

[Your Name]
